meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no coding experience. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup Without Coding (Beginner Case Study)
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement professional analytics without coding experience. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for SaaS founders.
content.answer_block: You don't need a CS degree to track user behavior. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for intelligent implementation, you can deploy event-driven analytics in minutes. This approach automates the technical heavy lifting, allowing you to focus on gathering data-driven insights to iterate your SaaS MVP faster.
content.intro:
The silent killer of most SaaS startups isn't a lack of features; it's the lack of data. You're launching into the void, guessing why users drop off, while your competitors are optimizing their funnels based on hard evidence. The barrier to entry—complex SDK integrations and code-heavy tracking—has kept you in the dark for too long.

Today, we shatter that barrier. We are moving from 'guessing' to 'knowing' by utilizing the latest AI-native development stack. By combining the infrastructure power of Antigravity with the autonomous reasoning of Claude Code, you will bypass the traditional development cycle and install a production-grade analytics layer without writing a single line of manual code.

steps.title: Define Your Metrics Strategy
steps.description: Before touching code, identify the three 'North Star' events that signify a user finding value. We need to focus on sign-ups, feature activation, and conversion events.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS Growth Lead. Create a JSON schema for tracking user signups, button clicks, and feature engagement events for a high-converting SaaS dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a tracking configuration file based on this schema and define the event triggers for my React frontend.'

steps.title: Initialize Analytics Provider
steps.description: Select a lightweight provider like PostHog or Mixpanel. We will use AI to handle the boilerplate connection logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the initialization code for a PostHog analytics provider within a standard Next.js environment, ensuring privacy compliance.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Inject the PostHog initialization script into the main layout component and configure environment variables.'

steps.title: Automate Event Capture
steps.description: Manually adding tracking to every button is tedious. We will write a wrapper that captures events globally.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a higher-order component or hook that automatically tracks clicks on all primary CTA buttons with specific data attributes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Apply the tracking hook to all components identified as buttons in the /src/components directory.'

steps.title: Validate Data Flow
steps.description: Ensure the data is actually firing. We use AI to write a test suite that simulates user behavior.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Playwright test script that navigates to the landing page, clicks the CTA, and verifies the analytics event payload.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Execute the Playwright test and report any failures in the event transmission pipeline.'

steps.title: Dashboard Visualization
steps.description: Turn raw data into actionable insights by creating a funnel view.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a guide for setting up a funnel report in PostHog that tracks the drop-off rate from landing page to sign-up completion.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze the current data logs and suggest the most critical funnel bottleneck based on the last 24 hours of activity.'

pro_tips: Always use data attributes (e.g., data-track='signup-btn') rather than CSS classes for tracking; CSS changes frequently, but your tracking logic should remain stable.
Implement 'Identity' calls immediately upon login to ensure you can attribute actions to specific user IDs, not just anonymous sessions.
Avoid over-tracking; focus on 5-7 key events that correlate with retention to prevent 'data noise' that leads to analysis paralysis.
Use a proxy for analytics traffic to prevent ad-blockers from stripping out your event data, ensuring higher accuracy for your funnel analysis.
Set up automated alerts for significant drops in conversion rate so you are notified of bugs before your users report them.
content.next_steps: Now that your tracking is live, spend the next 48 hours observing user behavior. Identify the single biggest drop-off point in your funnel and use Claude Code to suggest UI/UX improvements to fix that specific friction point.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start engineering for growth. You have the tools, the strategy, and the AI-powered leverage to outperform incumbents. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
